description: Angio is an amateur singer who resembles a young Jezz Torrent (as he appeared in GTA Vice City) and even sings one of his solos. The Judges initially insult William after his performance with two of the judges voting it "Shame" but after Lazlow begs everyone to do something controversial to get ratings, Hugh Harrison votes William "fame" and the other judges follow suit despite already making their votes. Hugh then asks William to punch Lazlow, which he does twice.
name: William Angio - Fame or Shame

description:
Johnny Joestar is the protagonist of Steel Ball Run and a minor character in JoJolion. He is the seventh JoJo of the JoJo's Bizarre Adventure series. 
 
Johnny is a young man of below-average height and slim to medium build. He has light eyes, and light, shoulder-length hair curling upwards at its ends. 
 
Johnny always wears a knit capW, printed with many small five-pointed stars. His hair sticks out of holes on either side of the top, taking a shape like short horns. A horseshoe hangs from the front; framing an image of the dark silhouette of the profile of a horse's reared head. 
 
Johnny wears similarly star-patterned pants; of a piece with footwear bearing spursW. He commonly wears a light-colored, hooded, short-sleeved top; printed with thick lines in the approximate formation of a harness (later, with patches in the shape of hearts adorning both shoulders). A long feather (bordered by black with one black spot at its tip) emerges from under the back of his collar; and he wears wrist cuffs patterned with stars and stripes. 
 
Johnny Joestar is a depressive ex-jockey, who clings to his hope of regaining the use of his legs through the Spin and then the Corpse Part throughout Steel Ball Run. 
 
Johnny is introduced as a distant paraplegic man whose career was ruined when he was shot in the spine after conceitedly antagonizing a passerby. After Johnny witnesses Gyro's Steel Balls and Spin, and how they enabled him to stand up on his feet for a moment, he becomes determined to follow him and make him teach the Spin, then after discovering the Corpse Part making his legs twitch, to acquire them.
name: Johnny Joestar
akas: ['ジョニィ・ジョースター', 'JoJo (by his fans)', 'Joe Kid (by his fans)']

description: Arthur Morgan is a central character and the main protagonist of Red Dead Redemption 2. Arthur Morgan was born circa 1863 to Beatrice and Lyle Morgan, somewhere in the northern United States. Around 1877, Arthur was found and picked up of the streets by Dutch van der Linde and Hosea Matthews.
name: Arthur Morgan

description: The mascot species of the Pokémon franchise, Pikachu is an Electric-type rodent that evolves from Pichu and can later evolve into Raichu. Pikachu has been extensively featured in nearly every piece of Pokémon media, and its popularity extends outside of its home series - it has been playable in every Super Smash Bros. game, painted on commercial airliners, and has its own balloon in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade.
name: Pikachu

description: His past is not known but he is known to the public as an important government official and the Director of FEMA. He was appointed to FEMA through Page's manipulation of an unnamed senator infected with the Gray Death, and went to work immediately. A cloak-and-dagger man, Simons was the first higher-up on the scene after JC Denton's capture of NSF operatives in Battery Park; his interrogation ended with Simons personally executing both prisoners.
name: Walton Simons

description: Peach (or Princess Peach) is a main protagonist in the Mario series. She's usually the damsel-in-distress of the games, who the players have to rescue, but she also has some important roles as the hero herself. She's the adorable and cute princess of the Mushroom Kingdom.
name: Peach

description:
"Post Tenebras Veritas" - Grayson's motto.
----

Grayson (his full name is never said. It is unknown whether Grayson is his first name, surname, or a pseudonym.) , formerly known as Sir Galahad is the main protagonist in The Order: 1886, a Knight of The Order and the third 'Sir Galahad', before being convicted of sedition against the establishment. He is a protégé of Sebastien Malory and after showing great skill and promise, he readily climbed up the internal ranks of The Order, and began to train his own protégé, Isabeau D'Argyll. Soon, Isabeau was knighted 'Lady Igraine', and they shortly developed romantic feelings for one another. Sadly, it was forbidden by law for them to have a relationship.

There's not a lot of info about Grayson's history in the narrative. The only implications of his origins are from an exchange with Sebastien where he states, "That was before even my time. The city walls had yet to come up".

Later, Sebastien dies and Grayson forms a pact with Alastair D'Argyll, calling each other 'brothers'. He however, made the mistake in sharing incriminating information without knowing that Alastair was a Half-breed. This forced Alastair to betray Grayson in favor of assuring the survival of his species.

Galahad is a serious figure, with undying loyalty to The Order. He's an expert tracker and hunter. However, under the service, self-doubt and concert exists. Jaded and numb to the violence he witnessed day-to-day, Galahad is world-weary with a calm, cool, professional demeanor.

After Sebastien's death, Galahad lost his calmness and turned vengeful, going ballistic on a rebel fighter until he spilled the leader's location. After learning the truth of Alastair's past, and not being able to tell the Council, he left the title 'Sir Galahad'.
name: Grayson / Sir Galahad

description: Mileena is a character in the Mortal Kombat series. A genetic experiment created by the sorcerer Shang Tsung, she is one of the adopted daughters of the Outworld Emperor Shao Kahn and one of his personal enforcers. She has served as an antagonist throughout the series.
name: Mileena

description:
Commander Shepard is the player character in BioWare's Mass Effect game trilogy: Mass Effect, Mass Effect 2, and Mass Effect 3. A veteran soldier of the Systems Alliance Navy military and N7-graduate of the Interplanetary Combatives Training (ICT) program, and the first human Citadel Council Spectre, Shepard works to stop "the Reapers", a sentient synthetic-machine race dedicated to wiping out all organic life.

Shepard's gender, class, first name and general appearance are chosen and customized by the player. Since the player can choose the gender of Shepard, much of the dialogue revolving around the character is gender neutral with only a few exceptions.
name: Commander Shepard (female)

description: Link is the main protagonist in The Legend of Zelda series. He's usually the hero of the game, and the player's avatar.
name: Link

description: Prince of Ylisse
name: Chrom

description:
Commander Shepard is the player character in BioWare's Mass Effect game trilogy: Mass Effect, Mass Effect 2, and Mass Effect 3. A veteran soldier of the Systems Alliance Navy military and N7-graduate of the Interplanetary Combatives Training (ICT) program, and the first human Citadel Council Spectre, Shepard works to stop "the Reapers", a sentient synthetic-machine race dedicated to wiping out all organic life.

Shepard's gender, class, first name and general appearance are chosen and customised by the player. Mark Meer provides the voice for male Shepards, while Jennifer Hale voices females. Since the player can choose the gender of Shepard, much of the dialogue revolving around the character is gender neutral with only a few exceptions.

Alan Shepard was the source of the character's name. Shepard's armour developed over the series, and was originally intended to be red-and-white. Though both genders were given equal importance during development, marketing felt there was a need for a single identifiable hero for promotion of the game. Various merchandise has been made, including several figurines. Shepard has appeared in cameo appearances in other Electronic Arts games, though they will not be appearing in any future Mass Effect ones.
name: Commander Shepard (male)

description: Garrus Vakarian is a turian, formerly part of C-Sec's Investigation Division. Like most turians, Garrus had his military training at fifteen, but later followed in his father's footsteps to become a C-Sec officer. He was responsible for the investigation of Saren Arterius, the Council's top Spectre, after the Alliance claimed Saren had gone rogue. Although Garrus was told that the investigation was over, he decided to defy the Executor's order and pursue another lead on his own. Eventually, Garrus attempts to join Commander Shepard's team to help defeat Saren and the geth.
name: Garrus Vakarian

description: Ragna the Bloodedge is the main protagonist of the BlazBlue series from Calamity Trigger to Central Fiction. He harbors a deep hatred of the Novus Orbis Librarium and aims to annihilate the organization. In turn, the NOL places the highest-ever bounty in history on his head. Ragna possesses a powerful form of ars magus (magic weapon) called the Azure Grimoire or BlazBlue which is central to the main conflict of the series.
name: Ragna the Bloodedge

description: Edelgard is a playable character and one of the main protagonists in Fire Emblem: Three Houses. She is the princess and the future emperor of the Adrestian Empire. In the Officers Academy, she is the leader of the Black Eagles.
name: Edelgard

description:
Trailer for the Kickstarter game " HIDE OR DIE " available soon. 
 
Asymmetric Horror has never been more frightening.
name: Trailer voice

description:
Josuke Higashikata 8 is the protagonist of JoJolion. He is the eighth JoJo of the JoJo's Bizarre Adventure series. 
 
Josuke is a young man afflicted with retrograde amnesia, lacking any memories prior to being discovered by Yasuho Hirose near the Wall Eyes in Morioh Town. He dedicates himself to discovering his former identity and those originally associated with him. 
 
Josuke is a young, handsome and physically fit man of above-average height. 
 
He wears a tasseled "Dixie cup" sailor cap (in multiple illustrations, it is adorned with the same palm insignia as Jotaro Kujo's hat). He wears a sailor suit, cut with a wide neck and above the navel, a neckerchief with a button resembling a slotted screw head, large emblems of an anchor on his right side and a compass rose on his left, fitted pants with a belt, and a pair of high-top athletic shoes. 
 
After partaking in an equivelant exchange to save his life. Josuke has segmented rocks which form a running stich going down his left side, passing through his eye. 
 
The tentatively named Josuke Higashikata is a quirky amnesiac determined to learn his true identity and his past. 
 
Josuke is afflicted by extensive retrograde amnesia, lacking memory of himself or events prior to meeting Yasuho Hirose, but retaining various degrees of knowledge. As such, Josuke is driven to discover who he was by investigating the circumstances of his presence in the beach prior to having been saved by Yasuho; that goal occupy nearly all of his activities and no action on his part isn't made without approaching him of learning the truth. That drive is sufficiently strong that he was willing to interrupt a rare moment with his love interest Yasuho to investigate Karera Sakunami. After learning about his past identity, Josuke set for himself the new goal of retrieving the Locacaca branch and use the fruit to heal Holy Joestar-Kira. He still demonstrates the same steadfastness in investigating the fruit and the whereabouts of the Locacaca Organization, with the addition of deferring more to Rai Mamezuku's opinions, deeming the plant appraiser's expertise about the fruit worth listening to. His determination to reach his goals is best illustrated when he willingly steps into a rain made deadly by Satoru Akefu's Stand, letting his body being pierced by raindrops including his head just to reach the hospital.
name: Josuke Higashikata 8
akas: ['東方 定助', 'Josuke Higashikata (given by Norisuke)']

description:
Talion is one of the two player-controlled protagonists in both Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor and Middle-earth: Shadow of War, alongside Celebrimbor. However, they share the same body. 
 
A descendant of the Northmen in Rhovanion, and raised in Minas Tirith, he is a loyal Ranger of Gondor, where he then rose the rank of Captain of the Black Gate. After the tragic demise of his family and himself, Talion was revived by Celebrimbor, an immensely powerful Wraith; where the two joined forces and began a quest for vengeance on those responsible for the death of his loved ones the Black Hand of Sauron.
name: Talion

description:
Felicia was first introduced in Darkstalkers: The Night Warriors as one of two female characters alongside Morrigan Aensland. She has appeared in every game in the series and in numerous cross overs including Marvel vs. Capcom 2: New Age of Heroes along with Morrigan, Baby Bonnie Hood and Anakaris. 
 
Felicia is a Catwoman who was taken in and raised by a Sister named Rose who gave her the name Felicia from the word Felicity. Despite her identity as a Darkstalker being exposed, she was not turned away by her as Felicia had feared. When Rose passed away, Felicia left her town hoping to be a musical star. 
 
She is an energetic and optimistic catgirl who enjoys singing, dancing and making friends with people. All she wishes for is peaceful coexistence with humans and doesn't resort to fighting unless she cannot avoid it. Because of this, she stands out among all the other Darkstalkers, many of whom are antihero at best. Her nice attitude has made some people tolerate Darkstalkers more and this gives her more motivation to persuade more humans to accept them since Felicia loves humans due to their personalities and open-mindedness, despite some not accepting Darkstalkers easily she doesn't give up her passion to make friends with the ones that care.
name: Felicia

description:
The Hammer of Sauron was a stretcher bearer during the War of the Last Alliance, who grew disillusioned and became seduced by Sauron's power after he came upon the Dark Lord's mace which had been dropped after Isildur defeated him. After that the Hammer realized that his life in helping wounded soldiers looking to find glory in war was wasted and thought that if they were so desperate to find glory on the battlefield then he would be more than happy to give it to them. 
 
Sometime after this epiphany, he joined a triumvirate consisting of himself, the Tower of Sauron and the Black Hand of Sauron. They became collectively known as the Black Captains.
name: The Hammer

description:
Agent 47 is the main character from the Hitman series and a highly skilled assassin, both in ability and in his techniques. He's a relatively emotionless assassin, but can now and then show some signs of morality, like when confessing his sins to Father Vittorio. Agent 47 doesn't work to gain money, but to have a purpose in life.

He is emotionally closed off to the suffering of others when appropriate on a contract, even if they remind him of himself at a younger age. His absolute highest priority is completing his contract. He will sacrifice innocents if he must, but he will kill the target. Vocally, he rarely raises his voice, choosing to speak in a calm and relaxed manner at all time. When speaking to another person, he can perfectly mask his true intentions and fool the other party with no effort. Agent 47 approaches his assassinations with professionalism but will, however, not show any hesitation or remorse when it comes to killing or silencing witnesses and other potential threats to his mission. 

His genetically engineered mind and body make competition next to nothing, and for this reason, Agent 47 is considered to be the world's most greatest assassin.
name: Hitman

description: Preston Garvey is a senior officer of the Commonwealth Minutemen, a volunteer defense militia operating in the Commonwealth in 2287. He is also available as a companion of the Sole Survivor.
name: Preston Garvey

description:
Ashley is a smart cookie. She's often found in the library, studying away with her nose in a book. 

After spending a lot of time with her study-partner Chris, Ashley wonders if he's interested in her as more than just a friend, but she soon snaps out of it - Chris would never ask her out anyway. 

Her overactive imagination means that she's jumpier than a paranoid kangaroo. She's not a fan of anything scary.
name: Ashley Brown

description:
Kiryu Kazuma is the former main protagonist of the Yakuza series from the first Yakuza up until Yakuza 6.

Kiryu is the honor-bound dreamer of the dark Yakuza world. He is not the type to judge others and is generally accepting of anyone who is friendly towards him. He lives by a strict code of honor and holds a very strong sense of justice. While he is extremely kind and goes out of his way to help civilians, he considers himself a Yakuza at heart, commonly using violence above words, so long as it is justified.

He generally lets his actions and mannerisms speak for him when possible. He tends to be the listening ear to the world, giving advice in response to his environment rather than seeking out conversations. He is wise but certainly not all-knowing, and generally means well for those around him, which makes him surprisingly pragmatic and well-liked.

Kiryu loves kids and shows himself to be a relatively competent caretaker to the children at Morning Glory Orphanage, with some help from Haruka Sawamura. He is genuine and understanding, bonding with the children through shared experiences. He tries to treat them all as fairly as possible and goes out of his way to keep them all safe and happy, whether that means putting on a wrestling match to cheer them up, or refusing to sell the land even in the face of a large sum of money.
name: Kazuma Kiryu
akas: ['Mister MesuKing', 'Uncle Kaz', 'Kazzy', 'Kazkaz', 'Kazu-chan', 'The Dragon of Dojima', 'Taichi Suzuki', 'Joryu']

description: Jak is the main protagonist of the "Jak and Daxter" video game series developed by Naughty Dog. He is a brave, agile hero with spiky blond hair and green eyes. As the series progresses, Jak initially appears as a silent and curious youth but gains a voice and a more complex personality. Throughout the games, Jak undergoes significant transformations, gaining various eco powers that enhance his abilities, such as super speed, strength, and dark and light eco powers. His journey is often accompanied by his witty and talkative sidekick, Daxter, who provides comic relief. Jak's character development, his evolving abilities, and the richly designed world of the series make him a memorable and iconic figure in the realm of action-adventure gaming.
name: Jak

description:
Mojo Jojo is a super villian and the main antagonist of Cartoon Network's The Powerpuff Girls franchise, both in the TV series and video games. He was Professor Utonium's former lab assistant and pet, being pretty sweet. Now, he's the Powerpuff Girls' arch-nemesis, wanting to destroy them with all his might. The urge to crush Townsville and the need to conquer the world keeps him going, no matter what. Mojo (who is a very smart) spends a lot of time creating new weapons or large robots. He has high-vocabulary use and long-lasting sentences.

When the professor created the girls, the Chemical X also hit Jojo, which gave him a huge brain, so large that it ended up sticking out of his head. He also was given new emotions, such as envy and anger. Soon, he became hateful towards the professor who started to ignore him and care more about the girls. Since then he spends his life wanting revenge. At first, he was isolated for a great period of time, becoming a loner. 

Mojo is a chimpanzee, but is often called an "ape", which irritates him.

Being very cunning, he often manages to play with the Powerpuff girls's naivety. Sometimes Mojo actually manages to find their weaknesses and use it against the girls in battle. Despite being very dark-natured, he lives another life outside of seeking revenge. He enjoys buying food instead of stealing it. He also likes to build model ships and values the sense of honor.

During one incident, Mojo Jojo actually manages to defeat the girls by outsmarting them. He nearly destroyed the girls, but the police managed to catch him and he ended up in jail, even angrier than before.
name: Mojo Jojo

description:
The protagonist of the Thief games is called Garrett, a master thief with a cynical view who wishes nothing more than to be left alone. He unwittingly becomes involved in a series of dramatic events, even though he just want to steal in peace. Growing up without any parents, he spent his childhood on the streets, having to pickpocket from people with money in order to survive. Later on a secret organization called The Keepers (dedicated to observe and maintain stability in the city) decided to recruit him. Garrett was given training in the arts of stealth, but soon found that it was way more profitable to make use of these skills as a thief than to continue working as an agent.

Garrett has a strong sense of professional pride as a thief, as well as self-interest. Being cynical and sarcastic, with a loyalty only to himself, he usually refuses to kill while on the job, stating that he's a thief and not a murderer.

During an incident, Garrett's right eye was pulled out from his socket. That's why he has a scar around the eye, as well as an experimental mechanical prosthetic. Weapons he uses includes a sword and bow, mines and the Blackjack.
name: Garrett

description:
Ana is one of the main characters in Rise of the Tomb Raider. She was Richard Croft's former girlfriend. Richard met Ana during a conference and found her very intelligent and interesting. Ana became very precious to Richard who said that having her in his life "Put new wind in his sails". Interested in his research, Ana helped with the organizing and ended up becoming his editor. However, Richard was a bit scared that Lara thought he was replacing Ana with Amelia Croft. The older Lara got, the more accepting of Ana she became, being happy that she made Richard happy.

That's why Lara becomes so disgusted and shocked when she finds out that Ana is the sister of an enemy, Konstantin, a mentally disturbed guy that works for Trinity and believes that he is carrying out God's will in everything he does. The siblings are looking for the Divine Source as Ana needs it to cure her sickness. 

At first, Ana tries to warn Lara to stay away from her father's research. When that doesn't work, she decides to drop the act. She has been manipulating Lara's family for years, doing everything in her power to get what she wants. Ana however, still seems a bit sentimental towards Lara and orders the Trinity forces to keep her alive. Throughout the story of Rise of the Tomb Raider, more about Ana is revealed, with an emotional twist at the end.

Ana's last name is never revealed.
name: Ana

description:
Victor Vran is the main character in the game Victor Vran. 
 
At the beginning of the game, Victor is travelling to the city of Zagoravia, destroyed and invaded by hordes of demons and undeads, in search of his friend Adrian, a fellow hunter, and Victor's best friend, the only one who knows his past. Despite being a member of Hunters Order, devoted to purge the world from demons and undead beings, Victor is not going to Zagoravia to answer the queen's call for help, as the city is overrunned by demons and many hunters had perished trying to oppose them, a disaster whose proportions are unprecedented.
name: Victor Vran

description:
Goro Majima is a recurring major character in the Yakuza series first introduced in the original Yakuza.

His unpredictability, ferocity in combat, and accomplishments as the captain of the Shimano Family earned him the nickname "The Mad Dog of Shimano". He is the former patriarch of the Majima Family, the founder and former president of Majima Construction, and the former vice-chairman of the Tojo Clan.
name: Goro Majima
akas: ['Mad Dog of Shimano', 'Lord of the Night', 'One-Eyed Demon']

description:
A beautiful bottle-blonde with a mischievous nature, fun is Jessica's raison d'être. First on the guest list at any party, if there's one thing she's not, it's shy.

Her fun-loving nature has certainly managed to turn Mike's head - he dumped Emily in order to get with her.

A childhood ballerina, Jessica once wanted to be a cheerleader... until she realized that it was going be quite a lot of work.
name: Jessica Riley

description: Isabella "Ivy" Valentine is a character in the Soul series of fighting games. She appears in every game since Soulcalibur.
name: Isabella 'Ivy' Valentine

description:
Faust is a recurring character in the Guilty Gear series. He is a medical genius gone insane and sane once more, and is probably one of the most unorthodox characters in the series. Having discarded his old identity, Faust is an itinerant healer dedicated to saving everyone he can to atone for his sins. 
 
Faust's extreme height and light weight give him a bizarre, tall, lanky physical appearance, and allows him to tower over every other character in the series—although he rarely does, due to his extremely low fighting stance. He wears a paper bag as a mask with one eyehole, which in one of his Taunts he takes off to reveal a glowing bald head and obscured face, and wields a giant scalpel as a weapon. 
 
His usual outfit consists of a collared, light-blue shirt with a black or yellow tie, green pants, and a stylized white coat with several sizable grey bolts on his shoulders and black belts. In -STRIVE-, Faust receives a major redesign: He wears simple light-blue scrubs, white gloves, a stethoscope around the neck, and mostly-white sneakers. It exposes an extremely skinny build and a stitched patch on the right side of his chest area. 
 
Going mad shattered whatever Faust's personality might have been, and even though he regained his sanity, a good deal of that wreckage is now permanent. And yet, contrary to his eccentric style and transportation methods, it is only in the battlefield that he can easily be described as "wacky".
name: Faust

description:
Jolyne Cujoh is the protagonist of Part 6 and the sixth JoJo of the JoJo's Bizarre Adventure series. 
 
Jolyne is a young woman of above-average height and slim to athletic build. 
 
Bold-eyed, she wears her hair in two "layers": A dark base including two large buns atop her head and a short length going down her neck; above which, lightly dyed or bleached, she keeps a plait or braid that winds around both buns, joining in a short length at back, and bangs that frame her face. 
 
Flighty, abrupt, and snappy with her elders, Jolyne in her teenage years was easily considered a delinquent and lost cause. Bitter at Jotaro for being absent from her life, she tried multiple times to catch her father's attention by committing multiple minor crimes and went to jail several times for them. Because of her father's absence, Jolyne was said to be dependent on the affection and approval of those around her, and she was ready to cover Romeo's accident, resulting in her being sentenced to 15 years in prison for it. 
 
After acquiring her Stand and being told by Jotaro of his love for her, Jolyne noticeably matures, becoming more courageous and confident. "Reviving" Jotaro, who fell into a coma due to Whitesnake stealing his Stand and memory, turns into her first priority.
name: Jolyne Cujoh

description:
Ori is a small white creature with a similar stance to a human or a bunny and has a cat-like tail and large black eyes with white pupils. They have two short horn-like antennae on top of their head, and underneath them there are larger ears, which droop when Ori is unhappy or weak. Ori's feet have small white hooves, as seen in their 3D model sheet. Their name is of Hebrew origin, literally meaning "my light"[1].

Ori's nature is largely up to the player, but they do have some distinct personality traits. Throughout the prologue sequence, it is seen that Ori's personality is akin to that of a young child, being very innocent and dependent on their caregiver. But as the story goes on, Ori's character shows a massive amount of determination; even in the face of death itself, Ori never once gives up hope. Ori is also very forgiving and kind-hearted, willing to help those who have entered the darkness. In general, Ori is incredibly altruistic and chooses to focus on the well being of the world around them before their own needs.
name: Ori

description: Naruto Uzumaki is a shinobi of Konohagakure's Uzumaki clan. He became the jinchūriki of the Nine-Tails on the day of his birth, a fate that caused him to be shunned by most of Konoha throughout his childhood. After joining Team Kakashi, Naruto worked hard to gain the village's acknowledgement all the while chasing his dream to become Hokage. In the following years, through many hardships and ordeals, he became a capable ninja regarded as a hero both by the villagers, and soon after, the rest of the world, leading him to achieve his dream and become the village's Seventh Hokage.
name: Naruto Uzumaki
akas: ['うずまきナルト', 'Seventh Hokage', 'Nanadaime Hokage', '七代目火影']

description:
Rosalina first showed up in Super Mario Galaxy, in the Comet Observatory. She watches over  the cosmos like a guardian, and is also the adoptive mother to the Lumas, the star-like little creatures that resides in the universe. Bowser was the one who attacked the observatory and sent it drifting into space without any power. When Mario and Rosalina met, they decided to help each other out. Rosalina promised to help Mario save Princess Peach if Mario in return could gather Power Stars in order to get the power back. 

At first, people thought that Rosalina and Princess Peach were related, and they did take that into consideration at first, but finally took another route in the end, making her another princess-like character in the Mario world.

Many gamers started to like Rosalina and her wise, kind and thoughtful personality. That's why the creators decided to include her as a playable character in future games. You can play as her in the Mario Kart series, Super Smash Bros, as well as in Super Mario 3D World.
name: Rosalina

description:
Solid Snake, real name David, also known as Old Snake, and briefly known as Iroquois Pliskin, or simply Snake, was a former spy, special operations soldier, and mercenary. Possessing an IQ of 180 and fluent in six languages,[1][5] he was known as "the Man Who Makes the Impossible Possible" and his exploits made him into a living legend among the military black ops. 
 
A product of Les Enfants Terribles project, Snake was a clone of world-renowned soldier Big Boss, along with his brothers Liquid Snake and Solidus Snake. Initially a Green Beret, Snake was later inducted into the High-Tech Special Forces Unit FOXHOUND in the mid-1990s while it was commanded by Big Boss. Repeatedly tasked with disarming and destroying the latest incarnation of Metal Gear, a bipedal nuclear weapon-armed tank, Snake would thrice avert potential nuclear catastrophe, becoming a famed war hero. 
 
Following the Shadow Moses Incident and subsequent smear campaign by the Patriots, the secret organization behind American politics, Snake was labeled a terrorist. He faked his own death in the Manhattan Tanker Incident, though he re-emerged years later to assist Raiden in the Big Shell Incident. During this time, his body entered a state of accelerated aging, due to intentional genetic changes made during the cloning process, causing his health to decline. 
 
In 2014, Snake performed his final mission, during which he defeated Liquid Ocelot and destroyed the Patriots. After one last encounter with Big Boss, Snake chose to live his remaining days in peace.
name: Solid Snake

description:
Lithariel is the daughter of Lady Marwen, Queen of the Shore. As an infant, she was the only survivor of a trading ship which fell prey to the raids of the Tribesmen. Queen Marwen adopted the infant as a daughter and she is now the first in line for the throne. 
 
No one questions Lithariel's authority. She grew up quickly among the Tribesman and has taken their values as her own. A tough warrior, she is resourceful and honorable to a fault. She has never surrendered in a battle, even when it cost those following her their lives.
name: Lithariel

description:
Abe is the central character in the Oddworld video game series, created by Oddworld Inhabitants. He first appeared in the 1997 game "Oddworld: Abe's Oddysee." Abe is a Mudokon, a species native to the planet Oddworld. He starts as a lowly floor waxer at RuptureFarms, the largest meat-processing plant on Oddworld, run by the ruthless Glukkons. The Mudokons are enslaved by the Glukkons and forced to work in dangerous and oppressive conditions.

Abe’s journey begins when he accidentally discovers that RuptureFarms plans to turn Mudokon slaves into a new line of meat products to save the failing company. Shocked and horrified by this revelation, Abe decides to escape from the factory and rescue as many of his fellow Mudokons as possible. His adventure involves solving intricate puzzles, sneaking past guards, and utilizing his unique ability to possess Sligs, the factory's enforcers.

Throughout the series, Abe evolves from an unsuspecting worker to a determined hero and liberator. He becomes a symbol of hope and resistance for the oppressed Mudokons. In "Oddworld: Abe’s Exoddus" (1998), he continues his mission, tackling new challenges and enemies as he aims to free his people from exploitation and enslavement.

Abe is characterised by humility, resourcefulness, and a strong sense of justice. His distinctive appearance, with blue-green skin, a topknot, and a stitched mouth, combined with his compassionate personality, has made him an iconic character in the world of video games. The Oddworld series has continued to evolve, with remakes and new entries keeping Abe's story alive and engaging for new generations of players.
name: Abe

description:
Hel is a Goddess and the keeper of the dead, lying judgement on souls to decide to is reborn. Beautiful and terrible she has both sides of two extremes. She's the daughter of the trickster God, Loki which means that her destiny was pretty much doomed to darkness from the very start. All-Father Odin had a prophecy that Hel and her siblings would end up creating Ragnarök, the battle that would end everything.  Wanting to prevent this, he cast them all into different realms and Hel got to rule over the realm of dead. 

When she was born, her face was cast half in light, half in shadow, meaning that she was both dead and alive at the same time. As the spirits of the kind-hearted, the sick, the elderly were brought to her, she cared for them, gave them comfort, yet those she deemed evil she mercilessly hurled into the frozen depths of Niflheim. 

One day, Hel will fulfill her prophecy, though it's still unknown if she will be a shadow of darkness or a force of light.
name: Hel

description:
The Big Bad Wolf, or Bigby Wolf as he calls himself in The Wolf Among Us, is the main protagonist and playable character in the game. Long time ago in the Homelands, Bigby was born with a group of seven cubs to Winter Wolf after being courted by the powerful North Wind. He was often teased by his brothers, but loved greatly by his mother until the day she died. When the others went out looking for their father, Bigby stayed to protect the corpse from bounty hunters. At that time, he was too weak to defend anything.

Angered by the loss, he swore vengeance, promising to eat something bigger as each day passed until he became large enough to confront his father. Seven times he tried, and seven times he failed, eventually burying his hatred and grief deep inside his heart. Around that time he became known as "The Big Bad Wolf" of legend. He often terrorized Little Red Riding Hood, attempting to devour her as he was stopped by the Woodsman who cut him open and filled him up with stones. That's also when the two of them became enemies.

After a series of events, Snow White tracks Bigby down and asks him to come to Fabletown. There, he becomes the sheriff with a duty to protect the Fables from mundies (humans) as well as themselves. Many people are still scared of the Wolf because of his past, and has a hard time trusting him. 

As a bit of a loner, however, he tries to prove to everyone day after day that he's not the same person anymore, and that he's capable of being the sheriff that everyone needs.
name: Bigby Wolf

description:
Ratbag the Uruk craves power without knowing what he would do with it. He cannot heft a heavy weapon over his head, but he will, stupidly perhaps, attempt to claw his way to the top of Sauron's Army by aligning with his people's great enemy, the Gravewalker. 
 
He has deluded himself into believing the Ranger will help him become a Warchief and, once he has power, he will be able to keep all he has achieved. His many scars bear witness to his ineptitude and history of failure. He is a craven little thing whose only chance of success is in spite of himself.
name: Ratbag

description:
Carley is one of the many main characters that appears in Telltale Games' The Walking Dead: Season One. She is a regional news reporter from Atlanta and a very skilled shooter. Carley's role is more active and she's never afraid to speak her mind. Having spent her time in war-zones as a correspondent, she presumably acquired basic knowledge of firearms and survival tactics that allows her to hold out during the outbreak.

Meeting Lee & Clementine in Macon, they later develop some kind of love relationship. Like the rest of the group, she cares about the members' welfare. Carley wants to save every survivor and give everyone a chance. Being very respectful and thoughtful, she reassures Lee that she will keep the secret to herself when he chooses to admit his past to her.

Not much is known about Carley's personal life, other than that she was raised Lutheran. The production crew drove to Macon to cover the Cherry Blossom Festival and that's when they were attacked by walkers. She was forced to watch her producer being eaten alive just a few feet away from her. Carley was saved by a guy called Doug.

Sadly, her life takes a turn for the worse as you get deeper into the story.
name: Carley

description:
Miranda Lawson is a human Cerberus Officer. As Jacob Taylor's informant, she gives him guidance and advice during missions, often via hologram. Miranda is very well traveled and has many contacts, extending from Citadel space and the Terminus Systems, to as far as the hostile Nemean Abyss. Her vast network of contacts may be explained by the fact Miranda is a known Cerberus operative, one who still has friends — or at least friends of friends — in the Alliance military. 
 
Miranda is a potential romantic interest for a male Commander Shepard.
name: Miranda Lawson

description: Mario Auditore (1434 – 1500) was the leader of the Italian Brotherhood of Assassins from at least 1476 to his death, a condottiero, and the ruler of Monteriggioni from 1454 until his death.
name: Mario Auditore

description:
Noel Kreiss is a playable character of Final Fantasy XIII-2, and a supporting character in Lightning Returns: Final Fantasy XIII.

When Noel was born in the Farseer tribe (that lived a nomadic life), he was the second to last child. After the Cocoon came crashing down, it's crystal shattering, the event left the world in ruins and warfare broke out that killed the majority of mankind.

When Noel travels back in time, he's the last remaining human being from 700 AF. He goes back to the past in order to try and change the outcome of his existence. He meets Lightning in Valhalla, who tells Noel about her sister and how she wants him to help them reunite while changing the future. Doing as he was told, he then goes to meet with Serah and quickly becomes her friend.

At first, Noel is cocky and confident, yet cheerful and soft-spoken. He's a self-sufficient fighter and a hunter that sadly suffers from amnesia thanks to the time paradox. Coming from a future where humanity is almost extinct, he has some trouble with his speech, so he tends to speak in a straightforward manner. The boy loves meeting new people and explore new places. Being very protective of others, he quickly starts caring for Serah and tries his best to protect her. Noel stands by his beliefs and morals, and as a remnant from his upbringing, values life and is reluctant to hurt or kill others unless necessary. This is the reason why he doesn't like Serah's boyfriend Snow, who has a very brash personality with the heads in the clouds.
name: Noel Kreiss

description:
Kratos was born in Sparta and became a fierce warrior. In a desperate moment during battle, he pledged his life to Ares in exchange for victory, becoming his servant. Ares, manipulating Kratos, caused him to slaughter his own wife and daughter, leading to Kratos’ deep hatred for the gods.

Fueled by rage and guilt, Kratos renounced Ares and embarked on a quest to destroy him, ultimately becoming the new God of War. His journey didn't end there; seeking revenge against the entire pantheon, Kratos waged war against the gods of Olympus, resulting in the destruction of the Greek world.

Years later, Kratos finds himself in the Norse realm, seeking a peaceful life with his son, Atreus. Despite his attempts to leave his past behind, he becomes entangled in the conflicts of the Norse gods. This new journey focuses on his relationship with Atreus and his struggle with his own violent nature as he faces gods and monsters of Norse mythology.
name: Kratos

description:
Emil, also known as No. 7, is a companion and deuteragonist in NieR, its remake, NieR Replicant ver.1.22474487139..., and a minor character in NieR:Automata.

A young boy that joins Nier's party towards the end of Act I, cursed with the ability to turn anyone who sees his eyes to stone. He is the younger twin brother of Halua.

In Act II, Emil reveals himself to be No.7, an ancient weapon created through human experimentation. He is ultimately transformed into a skeleton-like being after fusing with Halua, but retains his soul and gains new powers.

In NieR:Automata, Emil is revealed to have survived the events of NieR. A disembodied copy of Emil is briefly encountered inside the head of a machine, and is later seen attached to a moving sales cart where he offers his wares for the roaming androids of YoRHa. He recovers his memories with the androids' help and reveals he and countless other Emils were created by the original Emil to fight against the aliens.
name: Emil

description:
The G-Man is a mysterious and enigmatic character encountered several times in the Half-Life series. Referred to as a "sinister interdimensional bureaucrat",[3] he is known to display peculiar behavior and to wield powers far beyond those of a human. His identity and motives remain completely unexplained. His allegiance also remains unknown to this day. 
He plays the role of an overseer and eventual employer, watching over Gordon Freeman and other characters as the series progresses, at times helping or hindering them.
name: G-Man

description:
Mr. Satan, also known as Hercule, is the World Martial Arts Champion and the Earth's protector in the Dragon Ball Z animation and video games. His real name is actually Mark. When Mark was young, he attended a fighting dojo called "Satan Castle." Through a combination of considerable power and good luck, he quickly became world champion and was called "Mr. Satan" after the name of his dojo. During the Dragon Ball X time period, Mr. Satan won the champion title by beating normal earthlings in a time when Goku and the other fighters were battling on Namek. He was already famous and rich, which resulted in him also being a egomaniac. On television he kept talking big about his strength and that he was the best fighter in the world.

Using his fame as "the Hero who defeated Cell", he seduces and sleeps with girls, acting like a leech who always turns his head when seeing a pretty face. He is very protective of his daughter Videl however, and would not allow her to hang out at any boy's house unless they are tougher than him.

If you dig deeper down in Mark's personality, he's actually a decent and honorable man with a kind heart. He genuinely cares about protecting the innocent and fighting for justice the best as he can. He shows moments of great courage when his family and friends are threatened by evil.
name: Mr. Satan

description:
Dante is the primary protagonist of the Devil May Cry series created and published by Capcom. In the first four games, Dante is a private investigator and a demon hunting vigilante. He want to track down and kill those who murdered his mother and corrupted his brother. He is the son of Sparda who is a demon of great power. That's why Dante possesses numerous powers beyond that of any human. He can use a combination with a variety of weapons to accomplish his goals.

Named after the Italian poet Dante Alighieri, the character was designed to fit Devil May Cry game designer Hideki Kamiya's vision of a "cool and stylish" man, and his personality was based on the title character of the manga series Cobra. Dante's arsenal usually consists of firearms and melee weapons, including "Ebony and Ivory", twin handguns that never need reloading, as well as a variety of swords such as the "Rebellion" and "Force Edge", Devil Arms created by his father.

Dante has also become one of the most popular characters in gaming, having been recognized for his role in the Devil May Cry series and various characteristics that have attracted gamers. However, Dante's redesign and characterization in the reboot was the subject of controversy amongst gamers.
name: Dante

description:
Christa is a main character who first appears in Telltale Games's The Walking Dead: Season One, played by voice actress Mara Junot. She is much more serious than her boyfriend Omid, but loves him deeply and is concerned for the safety of the group. After Lee's death, Christa and Omid take up Lee's role as Clementine's guardians.

Little is known about Christa's life before the outbreak. She lived in a San Francisco apartment, and she and her boyfriend, Omid, were taking the "All-American Road Trip" when the apocalypse began...
name: Christa

description:
Bwana is the main character in The Journey Down. He runs a gas station with his friend Kito in the shady corner of Kingsport Bay, at the outskirts of St Armando. They struggle hard to get costumers since the place is a bit run-down. The gas station, also known as Gas 'n' Charter, has been run by the guys ever since they were mysteriously abandoned by their adoptive father.

Little does Bwana know that he's about to be thrown into a emotional and spine-tingling adventure when he one day is approached by a mysterious woman from Agibo University called Lina. She is seeking a lost book that may hold the illegal secret to reaching the Underland. Bwana takes this as a chance to get out of the misery, and convinces Kito to come with them. Together they embark on this mysterious journey and Bwana hopes that he can finally uncover the true fate of their long lost father, Captain Kaonandodo.
name: Bwana

description:
Trundle (League of Legends) is a devious troll with a mischievous streak. He can bend anyone to his will, as well as beat anyone and anything into submission. During a fight, he chills his enemies with his massive, frozen club and runs them through with shards of sharp ice. Trundle chases down anyone foolish enough to enter his domain and laughs as they bleed onto the tundra.

Trundle's warband once followed a weak leader, and were very foolish and cowardly. He feared that his kind would fall prey to other troll hordes. When he tried to win over the leader, but failing, he made up a story to fool others that if he found a weapon that would weird a great power, he would be the rightful leader of the warband. The trolls actually believed this story, but didn't think that he would succeed in his task.

Leaving the safety of his home, he went into the realm of the scary Ice Witch, hoping to find a weapon to prove his elaborate tale. Finally, he found his prize: a magical club of never-melting True Ice. Grabbing the weapon, the cold power run through him just as the Ice Witch appeared. Not wanting to die like this, she told the witch that she could use his troll army and that it would be a huge benefit for her.

Returning home, Trundle killed the leader after naming her weapon "Boneshiver" and took control of the warband. With Trundle leading the charge, the time of the trolls had finally come.
name: Trundle

description: Knuckles the Echidna from the Sonic the Hedgehog franchise by Sega.
name: Knuckles

description:
The character Doctor Ivo "Eggman" Robotnik is the man antagonist of the Sonic the Hedgehog franchise, created by Sega. Being a mad scientist, he constantly plans to conquer the world in order to fulfill his dream: to build his own Eggman Empire. He's the archenemy of Sonic, and wants to do everything in his power to stand as the last winner. Spme of Eggman's basic characteristics is his egg-shaped body, his sunglasses, his clothing in red, black and yellow, as well as his very large mustache.

Outside of appearing in almost every Sonic the Hedgehog video game, he's also a recurring character in other media such as comics, animations and novels.

Eggman loves machines and is often described as a genius with an IQ of 300. He doesn't care for others' opinions and never gives up on his desire to build the ultimate "utopia". Whoever interrupt his plans, becomes a prime threat and a target. Although Sonic has always ruined his evil plans, Eggman holds a secret respect for his determination.

Many of the robots in the earlier games are actually animals that Robotnik trapped in mechanical outfits. These are the same animals that Sonic frees at the end of each level.
name: Dr. Eggman
akas: ['Dr. Robotnik', 'Eggman', 'Robotnik', 'ドクター・エッグマン', 'ロボトニック']

description:
Circus Baby (commonly referred to as Baby) is the secondary antagonist of the Five Nights at Freddy’s series and the animatronic mascot of Circus Baby's Pizza World invented by Afton Robotics, LLC and made for Fazbear Entertainment, Inc.

Baby is haunted by the soul of Elizabeth Afton, who was the daughter of the serial killer William Afton. However, during the completion ending, Elizabeth's soul was presumably set free.
name: Circus Baby

description:
Geralt of Rivia (or Geralt z Rivii in Polish) is a witcher and the main protagonist of The Witcher game series by CD Project RED. However, he originally comes from a series of books with the same name, written by Andrzej Sapkowski. As a witcher and a monster hunter, you can hire him for anything, as long as he gets paid for it. Geralt possesses nearly superhuman abilities and is an expert with swords. During The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt (in 1272), Geralt's mentor Vesemir says that he is "nearly a century old".

Geralt's mother, Visenna, left him with the witchers in Kaer Morhen, where he grew up. During this time, to appear more trustworthy to future clients, young witchers were encouraged to make up surnames for themselves. Geralt wanted to pick "Geralt Roger Eric du Haute-Bellegarde", but Vesemir saw the names as silly and unprofessional, leaving him with Geralt, and Geralt only. Even though he didn't come from Rivia, "Of Rivia" was a practical alternative as a last name. Geralt adopted the name and even started to talk with a Rivian accent so that he would appear more authentic. Except from that name, the dryads gave him the nickname "The White Wolf".

During the "Trial of the Grasses", Geralt survived numerous mutations, and he was one of the few who survived. That's why his hair is white (no pigment), and his senses better and stronger than others. When his witcher training was over, he embarked on his horse called Roach (giving the same name to every one of his horses), to become the slayer of monsters.

For his services, as a reward, Geralt wanted the unborn child of princess Pavetta. However, when he found out that it was a girl, he decided not to take her. But fate caused Geralt and Pavetta's daughter Ciri to cross paths multiple times, and after the death of queen Calanthe (Ciri's grandmother), The White Wolf decided to take care of the girl. He soon came to love her as his own daughter.

Geralt's love of his life is called Yennefer of Vengerberg, and he also has a really close friend that goes by the name Dandelion. He's a poet and a bard with a wild imagination and a love for girls.
name: Geralt of Rivia

description: Delilah is the deuteragonist of Firewatch and the supervisor of Henry, the game's protagonist. Henry remains in nearly constant contact with her via radio and can be reached at any time she or Henry starts a conversation or Henry finds something worth of reporting to her, except during sections taking place in the cave. As the game progresses, increasing doubt is shed on Delilah, her relationship with Henry, her affiliation and what she potentially knows. As such, the player has the choice when making discoveries to report to her or not. The player never meets Delilah in person, leaving her appearance a mystery.
name: Delilah

description:
Good looking, smooth as silk, and silver-tongued, Mike is the resident ladykiller. He's had more girlfriends than he's had hot dinners, which can cause a few problems when a few of them are in his immediate circle of friends...

Mike is a man of action. He's always the first to volunteer, the first to make a suggestion. With a narcissistic streak, there's no denying that he likes the attention that comes with being the man who stands up and can get things done.
name: Michael Munroe

description:
Morrigan Aensland is a fictional character in the video game series Darkstalkers. She first appeared in Darkstalkers: The Night Warriors. She is an anti-hero and a main protagonist alongside Demitri Maximoff. 
 
Morrigan Aensland is a succubus who was born in 1678 in a region of Scotland. Belial foresaw that Makai would be exposed to the crisis of perishing soon. Someone, a great Life Form, would be needed to maintain the unbalanced world of Makai. He foresaw that one with such power would not be born in the family of Aensland for another 1,200 years. Knowing this, he decided to leave the future of the Aensland family in Morrigan's hands. To Belial, the future of the Aensland family was the same as the future of Makai. 
 
Beautiful, sexy, confident, conceited, and playful, Morrigan may be called many things given that she is a succubus, but she is not truly evil despite her heritage as a demon of the Makai realm. Instead she is friendly and approachable, perhaps in part due to her frequent visits to the material world to pursue her hedonistic tendencies and has found herself willingly aligned with the forces of good on many occasions. Morrigan is always out to satisfy her hunger and will often shirk her responsibilities as the current head of the Aensland estate to find something more engaging.
name: Morrigan Aensland
akas: ['モリガン=アーンスランド', 'Morigan Ānsurando']

description: Miles "Tails" Prower, from the Sonic the Hedgehog franchise by Sega.
name: Tails

description:
This character is from TES 5 Skyrim mod called Bruma. 
 
A khadjit who lives in Bruma. Originally from Elsweyr Dar'taqto has lived most of his life in Cyrodil. He says that he moved from Elsweyr so long ago that does not rememder what the reason was. Maybe he just doens't want to tell the reason. 
 
Dar'taqto seems to have warm feelings towards Elsweyr. He can even tell the protagonist "You are to Dar'taqto as the sun is to Elsweyr... I mean - a good friend". 
He says that he would like to become emperor one day. It seems that he is dissapointed in current emperor. 
 
Dar'taqto gets banned from Bruma but the protagonist can help him by taking him along as follower. 
 
He fights enemies with two battle axes. Dar'taqto wears light armor. 
 
WARNING: The outfit on the picture is not his original outfit. The picture is taken at Skaal Village, Soltheim.
name: Dar'taqto

description: Nick Valentine is a synth detective and potential companion living in Diamond City. He is found in Vault 114 in 2287.
name: Nick Valentine

description:
Elizabeth is a character and A.I. companion in BioShock Infinite.
With the game set in 1912 on a sky city (named Columbia) , Elizabeth is to take over the city once its current leader (or "Prophet"), Father Comstock, dies. Elizabeth can make and exploit "tears", rips to other universes, allowing her to move between them and/or bring in items from them. To prevent her from leaving Columbia, a remote "Siphon" is made which limits her powers, and she is locked in a tower under study and guarded by a giant mechanical bird known as Songbird.
name: Elizabeth

description:
Alan Wake was born and raised in New York City. He developed a passion for writing at a young age, eventually leading to a successful novelist career. His most famous works are thriller novels, which have earned him a large and dedicated following. Despite his success, Alan struggles with writer's block and personal issues, including a strained relationship with his wife, Alice.

In an attempt to overcome his writer's block, Alan and Alice take a vacation to the idyllic town of Bright Falls. However, their peaceful retreat quickly turns into a nightmare when Alice disappears under mysterious circumstances. Alan discovers pages of a manuscript he doesn't remember writing, detailing events that start to come true in real life.

As he searches for Alice, Alan encounters dark supernatural forces known as the "Taken," shadowy entities controlled by a malevolent presence called the "Darkness." He learns that the town and its surroundings are influenced by this dark power, which has trapped him in a nightmarish reality where his own writing becomes a weapon and a tool for survival.

A crucial figure in Alan's journey is Thomas Zane, a poet who lived in Bright Falls decades earlier and had a similar encounter with the Darkness. Zane’s writings and his struggle against the Darkness provide Alan with clues and guidance on how to fight supernatural forces.

In the end, Alan realizes that to save Alice and stop the Darkness, he must make a significant sacrifice. He writes a conclusion to the horror story he is trapped in, allowing Alice to escape while he remains in the dark, nightmarish world beneath Cauldron Lake. This act of self-sacrifice shows his character's deep love for his wife and his determination to end the cycle of terror.
name: Alan Wake

description:
Clementine, affectionately nicknamed Clem or Sweet Pea, is one of the main characters in the game The Walking Dead, by Telltale Games. She's not playable in season one, but the story still involves heavily around her. She is kind, polite and very mature for her age, acting as a moral compass of the group. Clementine is remarkably smart and good when it comes to improvising. She can be very practical during dire situations. She's more careful than other kids as she is aware of the current events going on. However, despite her maturity, Clementine is still relatively innocent in the way children are.

During the outbreak, Clementine ends up hiding in her tree-house after her babysitter goes missing, and her parents doesn't return home from vacation. A guy called Lee (the playable character) soon finds her and decides to take her with him after having to battle the babysitter who died and became a zombie. Together they embark on a long journey to find safety and eventually becomes very fond of each other.

After losing the people closest to her, Clementine becomes hardened and more mature in her outlook, but still bravely overcomes the dangers of a post-apocalyptic world head-on and tries her best to maintain some of her previous faith in humanity, hanging on to what Lee taught her and her own values.
name: Clementine

description:
Big-Foot/Sasquatch the names don't matter but the legend remains the same. Thought to be nothing more than superstition by Native's of the land; the Big-Foot is half man, half beast in the form of a hairy ape. 
 
The beast is rumored to have the strength of ten men and the ferocity of a grizzly bear, in Terrordrome the Sasquatch is neither friend nor foe, it simply wants to be left in peace but the chaos going on around it has caused it to awaken from it's slumber and take the fight back to the enemy.
name: Sasquatch

description:
If you play The Walking Dead: Season One by Telltale Games, you will meet Omid who is one of the many main characters. Omid is very friendly, kind-hearted and loving. He cares deeply for his girlfriend, Christa. Even though the world is going to hell, Omid has a positive view on life and tries to stay strong for others around him. He is often seen as the comic-relief of the group, and tries hard to lift the spirits of his companions. However he's not into heavy and troubling topics and tries to avoid it as much as possible. Additionally, Omid is assumed to be really intelligent, and is really into Civil War history. 

Before the zombie apocalypse, him and Christa shared an apartment in San Francisco. They owned a pet cat and enjoyed to travel. When the dead started to walk on the earth, they were on an "all American road trip". They went to Barstow, and had a Vegas Weekend together. Not much else is known about their past lives.

Christa and Omid later becomes Clementine's guardian & swears to protect her.
name: Omid

description:
Grand Arch Master of the Order of One. 
 
Dupoint Lucroix is the Grand Master trying to keep order while his assistant Alexander seeks to use the evils of mankind to better himself.
name: Grand Master Dupoint Lucroix

description:
In Telltale Games' The Walking Dead, there's many main characters and one of them is Kenny. As a kid he was raised by his father, who taught him how to repair broken cars. That knowledge soon extended to boats due to his love of the water and traveling. As an adult, Kenny became a commercial fisherman, hunting for the snapper fish and yellow fin. During a fishing trip, he found a sick octopus and decided to bring it to a veterinarian's office. There he met and fell in love with Katjaa. They got married and later had a son called Kenny Jr, also nicknamed Duck. The family were returning from Memphis, Tennessee after visiting Katjaa's sister when the outbreak started.

Meeting Lee and Clementine along the way, they decided to stick together for a better chance of survival. Kenny acted as a co-leader of Lee's group (but eventually acknowledged Lee as the true leader) but his short temper and failure to think things through often got the better of him. However, Kenny is also shown to be selfless and kind, being willing to give his life to save someone else's. 

Kenny often went sailing to the open sea for months at a time, separated from his family, which he admits to be a fact that he deeply regrets after his son gets bitten by a zombie and his wife commits suicide because of the loss of Duck.

Kenny displays numerous stereotypes of a redneck and suffers from insomnia.
name: Kenny

description: Booker DeWitt (Troy Baker), the player protagonist, is a disgraced former agent of the Pinkerton National Detective Agency. As a soldier in the 7th Cavalry Regiment, he had performed brutal acts against native American Indians at the Battle of Wounded Knee to defend his own honor; these acts left him emotionally scarred, leading to excessive drinking and gambling and earned him the title "White Injun" due to his tendency to scalp those he killed. He was given an opportunity to be baptised and start anew, but ultimately declined. He was subsequently dismissed for behavior beyond the acceptable bounds of the Agency,[2] but considers his actions with the Pinkertons in quelling labour strikes to be among his many sins. He continues to work as a private investigator from New York City, referring to himself as an "independent contractor". Outwardly, he cares little for the extraordinary, provided that it does not interfere with his ability to do his job; internally, he is disturbed by both his role in the events at Wounded Knee and recurring visions of New York City under attack from the air. Booker is skeptical of faith, unwilling to accept the idea that he can be absolved of his sins by embracing religion, as he considers his sins to be so extreme as to demand a penance rather than forgiveness.
name: Booker DeWitt

description:
Born in april 9 1967 
lives in a Penthouse in Las Vegas, Nevada. 
Saved the world from alien invasion multiple time, work with the EDF (Earth Defence Force)
name: Duke Nukem

description: Samuel is a minor character in The Curse of Dwight Periwinkle.
name: Bouncer Samuel

description:
Matt is the high school's star linebacker, but he isn't necessarily the sharpest tool in the box. He's hoping for a sports scholarship to a top college, because his GPA isn't going to cut the mustard.

He mostly plays the part of the big friendly giant - but when Matt stays red, it's best to stay out of his way...

Matt is fiercely loyal to his girlfriend Emily... maybe even too loyal. Their relationship could be described as being a little one-way: when Emily says something, Matt does it.
name: Matthew Taylor

description:
The Skeleton King is a boss character found in Diablo 3, formerly known as King Leoric. Even though he was born in Kehjistan, Leoric decided to take his family and travel into the lands of Khanduras, where he in the name of the Zakarum, declared himself as the king. At first, the people of Khanduras disliked the idea to have him as a ruler. However, as time passed, they grew to like and respect him, thanks to his purity of heart and deed.

Unknown to Leoric however, his seat of power was the resting place of Diablo. He used the people too free himself from imprisonment, and took Leoric as his host for his evil deeds. Leoric tried to fight against it for a long time, but initially failed as Diablo stripped his soul from all honor and virtue. The people of Khanduras started to see a change in their king and how he became deformed and insane. He soon became cruel, and didn't hesitate to kill anyone or anything who dared to oppose him. Leoric even decided to execute Queen Asylla, his own wife. That's when he got the new nickname: "The Black King". 

One day, Leoric's lieutenant, the knight Lachdanan, came back from war, only to find his king mad and crazy, accusing people for kidnapping his son. Lachdanan begged for an explanation, but Leoric simply spat at him, calling him and his men traitors. With sadness in his heart, Lachdanen killed his king and Leoric cursed the knights just before his death.

King Leoric got a proper burial, but Diablo didn't want to give him that peace and raised him from the dead as a powerful undead warlord called the Skeleton King.
name: The Skeleton King

description:
Rigby is one of the protagonists of Carton Networks' Regular Show, as well as a playable character in the online game Fusion Fall. He's a 23 year old brown raccoon with short, spiky hair and made his debut in the pilot episode, working in the park as a groundskeeper. Rigby received the nickname "The One Cheek Wonder", after a friend damaged his butt in a "fun" fight. The raccoon had to get surgery that resulted in his behind looking like it only had one butt cheek.

Described as a "wild adult", he often becomes frightened, confused, frustrated or sad very easily. He can also be very impulsive, impatient and arrogant at times, his behavior causing him to act more like a child. Rigby cares a lot about his friends and tries to help the best he can.

Rigby is prone to overly dramatic reactions to the simplest things and often tries to skip work and disobey orders. However, as the series progressed, he has become a bit more responsible and thoughtful, tending to complain less and even accomplishing tough tasks.

He's terrified of horror movies.
name: Rigby

description:
Zinyak is an extraterrestrial warlord and the bad guy in Saint's Row IV. He's the leader of the alien race known as the Zin. Sounding very posh, he speaks with an upper class English accent and is a big fan of literature. It was actually the love for books that brought him to Earth. Zinyak often makes references from famous work, such as Macbeth and Edgar Allan Poe. 

During the events of the game, he invades Earth and comes through the ceiling of the White House where he abducts Kinzie Kensington and Shaundi before disappearing. He also defeats the Boss, who is now the President of the United States, and puts him/her into a virtual simulation based on their worst fears. 

Zinyak live streams everything to his planet where all the Zin can watch how he slowly but surely kills the humans and erases mankind. His goal is to take over, not really thinking that anyone on Earth can be strong enough to stop him.
name: Zinyak

description:
As The Tower's evil has grown, so has his stature. He is trapped inside armor that will not grow with him, and his agony is exquisite. He was once a Númenórean and as he can recall, he either built a tower, or was locked inside one. 
 
His appearance is horrific. To stand near him is to feel the revulsion he feels for himself. Even his fellow Captains despise The Tower, although they have come to rely on his great corrupting evil. His propensity for slaughter is unmatched. He makes his victims feel his pain, and he revels in it.
name: The Tower

description: Peach Wilkins is a resident of Neptune's Bounty and seems to be one of the leaders of Frank Fontaine's smuggler group.
name: Peach Wilkins

description:
Jam Kuradoberi is a character in the Guilty Gear series. She is of Chinese descent, and is one of the few characters in the game who fights bare-fisted, supplemented with Ki force. 
 
Jam stands 5'4" tall, with long brown hair worn in a waist-length loop and secured at the top of her head with a "Pokka" Oolog tea can. She has brown eyes, fair skin, and a very athletic physique. 
 
Jam's costume is a tight-fitting Chinese-styled dress of red silk and black trim. It has no sleeves and falls to about the top of her thighs and is cut high over the hip. Under this she wears a second skirt, which is white with red trim and about the same length as the red dress (but it covers her hips, which the red dress doesn't). On her arms she wears wide "sleeves" made from red silk with black trim. These sleeves are held on by straps at her upper bicep. On her feet Jam wears high-heeled red boots with black trim. 
 
Jam is a woman who often jumps to conclusions and is quick to make decisions, while taking the consideration of others into account. In Guilty Gear XX, she immediately got into fights with the other characters, suspecting them as the arsonists who burnt her restaurant down. Jam also takes great pride in her Chinese heritage and skill in both fighting and cooking, often using food as a reference. Overall, she is a very energetic person who is sometimes a little silly, but still tries to establish friendships.
name: Jam Kuradoberi

description: Daxter is a central character in the "Jak and Daxter" video game series. Originally a human, Daxter is transformed into an ottsel, a fictional hybrid of an otter and a weasel, after falling into a pool of Dark Eco. Known for his witty and outspoken personality, Daxter serves as Jak's loyal sidekick and best friend. Despite his small stature, he is resourceful, brave, and provides comic relief throughout their adventures. Daxter's unique transformation and humorous nature make him a memorable and indispensable part of the duo.
name: Daxter

description:
Tommy, an Italian-American, is portrayed with a tall, swarthy, handsome appearance, combed dark brown, almost black hair, and a constant five o'clock shadow. He first appears wearing a light blue-green Hawaiian shirt with dark blue palm trees printed on it, a gold pearl necklace around his neck, a gold watch around his left wrist, and a pair of blue jeans and white sneakers. This outfit becomes known as Tommy’s "street" clothes. As the game progresses, Tommy is offered more wardrobe options: he obtains a blue-purple pastel suit with the sleeves rolled up over a black dress shirt and black loafers, as well as the option of a dark pinstripe suit labeled as "Mr. Vercetti" (which bears similarities to Tony Montana's uniform). A light green costume is available to perform a bank robbery, complete with a hockey mask, and is even able to wear the uniform for an allied gang, among many others.

Tommy Vercetti, in several ways, exhibits characteristics of fictional drug lord Tony Montana from the 1983 film Scarface. This coincides with the heavy themes and appearance of the movie that has been implemented into Vice City. Among these characteristics, his exile from his old home (Liberty City), his rise to power (acquiring property and wealth in the city, and a mansion which also sports an interior similar to that of Montana’s mansion). Tommy is also a hired assassin, has killed his own collaborators (Lance Vance), taken over his temporary boss’s business (Ricardo Diaz) and rebelled against his former leader (Sonny Forelli), as Tony Montana had. The only notable difference is that the final gunfight in Montana's mansion sees Montana eventually killed, whereas the final gunfight in Tommy's mansion sees Tommy managing to single-handedly take down his captors and survive.
name: Tommy Vercetti

description: Rintarou Okabe (岡部 倫太郁) is a self-proclaimed mad scientist and the main protagonist of Steins;Gate. He is a student of Tokyo Electronics University and the founder of Future Gadget Laboratory, a small research laboratory devoted into creation of future gadgets for the betterment of mankind. He also likes to pose as an agent working against a certain "Organization" which he thinks is always after him, as he believes this kind of image gives him a cool and mysterious aura. He is one of the (accidental) inventors of the Future Gadget #8, Phone Microwave, which has the ability to send data back in time.
name: Rintaro Okabe

description:
Ursula is a 32 year old female Newman and a supporting character in Phantasy Star II & Phantasy Star Portable 2. Newmans are a race evolved from humans in the universe. They were created for research purposes, but eventually became a race of their own. She is a brilliant and sharp woman with many talents. She spent many years in the Alliance Military Force, where she met Kraz Muehler. They fell in love and decided to marry each other.

After being an instructor of AMF, she became a leader of Little Wing and her beauty mixed with her talents sent out a message of respect and dignity. Ursula however, often hides her feelings, leaving her with many regrets about her past. She's often sad about a lot of choices and thinks about them a lot. 

Ursula is also a fashion designer.
name: Ursula

description:
Sejuani is a playable character in League of Legends. As a child, the leader of the Winter's Claw watched her tribe die, one after one. She was the only one of her siblings that survived to become ten years old. Yet she feared for her life, sure that she would be the next to die. Becoming desperate, she sought spiritual counsel from the tribe's mystic. She told Sejuani that she one day would conquer and unite the tribes of the Freljord. However, the mystic left out the event of her death.

Now, when she had absolute faith in her destiny, Sejuani pushed herself to extremes and got hardened that way. She turned pain into power and hunger into an encouragement. She walked into blizzards without food or furs, challenged the strongest warriors, one after another until she became the leader. The tribe then grew stronger, even though she learned that being cold and unforgiving was the only way. 

A peace offering from Ashe's tribe insulted Sejuani greatly. Ashe told her that if they united the clans, the Winter's Claw would never go hungry again. Sejuani just simply hated the idea of getting that luxury, and started to despise Ashe's tribe. After telling her people that charity would bring only weakness, she burned the given supplies and sent the tribe back with one message:  the Winter's Claw would prove to the Avarosan that only the strong deserve to survive in the Freljord. After that event, Sejuani rode out with her warriors to inflict many painful lessons to others.
name: Sejuani

description: Kurisu Makise is a genius girl who graduated from university at the age of seventeen. She is a research member in Victor Qondria University's Brain Research Program and her research on the brain's memory retention system has earned critical acclaim in the scientific community. She later joins the Future Gadget Lab as Member 004 after a series of encounters with Rintaro Okabe, where she helps to create the Time Leap Machine, a microwave oven capable of sending memories 48 hours back in time. She has a myriad of nicknames, most of which are invented by Rintaro, much to her dismay.
name: Kurisu Makise
akas: ['Christina', 'Assistant', 'The Zombie', 'Celeb Seventeen', 'Celeb Sev', 'KuriGohan and Kamehameha', 'Mother of Time Machine']

description:
Ryu Hayabusa is the protagonist in the Ninja Gaiden series developed by Team Ninja and published by Tecmo. He also appears in almost every mainline games of the Dead or Alive series. 
 
As a ninja, Ryu ruthlessly kills his enemies without mercy, executing them in a very cold, technical and calculative fashion. His rigorous upbringing in tandem with the many hardships and tragedies he has suffered, has forced Ryu to harden his emotions and disciplined his skills to perfection. He is undaunted by the lives he must take in order to protect the balance within the world, knowing that the balance of the world is more important. 
 
Ryu knows life following the ninja path can be both cruel and short, especially for one of the Dragon Lineage. He has already felt first hand the brutality of this life; watching Kureha, his best friend murdered right in front of his own eyes, his clan massacred and he himself killed as he tries to save his village. Ryu endures these hardships with his unstoppable will, an indomitable spirit shown in his exploits as he overcomes every challenge and obstacle, surviving even the trauma of death to return and exact his vengeance. 
 
Though Ryu cares little for his enemies' well being, he still respects them on a warrior basis, even having mutual respect with some adversaries such as Genshin. Outside of battle, Ryu is modest and refined, he is popular among the local residents in his village. Not only as a strong role model for ninja in training, but he is heralded for being kind and gentle, as he cares for the safety of every resident in his home. This is due to the fact that each Hayabusa clansman from Jonin to cadet is cherished as family.
name: Ryu Hyabusa
akas: ['the Super Ninja', 'the Singular Super Ninja', 'Dragon Ninja', 'the Modern Day Ninja', 'the Ultimate Ninja']

description: Mayuri Shiina (椎名 まゆり), is Rintaro Okabe's childhood friend and companion. She works part-time at a maid cafe called May Queen Nyan-nyan as a costume maid and occasionally makes cosplay outfits. She is also the second member of Rintaro's Future Gadget Laboratory.
